description:
You are a Site Reliability Engineer in a Network Operations Center (NOC) building a new incident response dashboard. During an outage, operators waste precious minutes manually unchecking benign servers in the Graphite legend to find the ones actually under load. You need a dashboard that automatically filters out low-traffic sensors, ranks the top CPU consumers, excludes a known maintenance node, and sorts disk I/O peaks for instant legibility.

Create and save a Graphite dashboard named 'Exception Monitoring' containing the following four graphs:

1. 'Top Compute Consumers': Use highestAverage to display the two nodes with the highest average CPU utilization across all compute resources (EC2 and RDS).
2. 'Critical Web Traffic': Use averageAbove to display only web traffic sensors with an average speed exceeding 60.
3. 'Sorted Disk Peaks': Use sortByMaxima to display all EC2 disk write metrics, sorted so the instance with the highest absolute peak appears first in the legend.
4. 'Active EC2 Fleet': Use exclude to graph EC2 CPU utilization while filtering out 'instance_2' using a substring match.
